Title: Advances in plant-derived C-glycosides: Phytochemistry, bioactivities, and biotechnological production

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT family, GT1 (GT-B superfamily), UGT708 family
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: C-glycosylation step forming C-C glycosidic bond
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding C-glycosides; flavone C-glycosides after dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sugar from UDP-sugars to acceptor phenolics through a C-C bond, catalyzing C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanones, dihydrochalcones, and benzophenones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms C-glycoside products / immediate precursors of flavone C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCGT was the first plant C-glycosyltransferase discovered. 2. It belongs to type I CGTs in the UGT708 family. 3. It catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanones and related phenolics to support C-glycoside formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reported functional enzyme characterization in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-designated core pathway gene summarized as representative CGT; type I CGTs contain a conserved DPF motif near the N-terminus.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT family, GT1 (GT-B superfamily), UGT708 family
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: C-glycosylation step forming C-C glycosidic bond
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C-glycosylated 2-hydroxyflavanone products leading to flavone C-glycosides after dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanone acceptors using UDP-sugars
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors of flavone C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCGT is a type I plant CGT from maize. 2. It uses 2-hydroxyflavanone as substrate. 3. It is cited as an example of early functional plant CGTs for C-glycoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reported functional enzyme characterization in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review also states UGT708A6 can show O-glycosylation activity in addition to C-glycosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of multiple phenolic acceptors with preference for UDP-Glc over UDP-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeCGTa is a functional plant CGT from buckwheat. 2. It accepts multiple phenolic substrates. 3. It prefers UDP-Glc to UDP-Xyl.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reported functional enzyme characterization in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure mentioned among plant CGTs obtained according to review conclusion.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of multiple phenolic acceptors with preference for UDP-Glc over UDP-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FeCGTb is a functional plant CGT from buckwheat. 2. It accepts multiple phenolic substrates. 3. It prefers UDP-Glc to UDP-Xyl.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reported functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxynaringenin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmCGT is a type I CGT from soybean. 2. It catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxynaringenin. 3. D85A mutation abolished activity, supporting key role of the conserved DPF-associated residue.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assay; mutagenesis (D85A) in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes D85A mutant lost C-glycosylation activity, supporting functional importance of DPF motif.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type I CGT; benzophenone/xanthone/simple aromatic C-glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 35 substrates including benzophenones, xanthones, and simple aromatics
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regio- and stereospecific C-glycosylation of a broad range of phenolic substrates, preferring UDP-Glc over UDP-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MiCGT displays broad substrate promiscuity, acting on 35 compounds. 2. It catalyzes regio- and stereospecific C-glycosylation. 3. It has been used for synthesis of new benzophenone C-glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assay; biocatalytic synthesis in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states MiCGT also exhibits O-glycosylation activity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly transfers sugar to flavone backbone at C-6 of apigenin and luteolin using UDP-Glc or UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GtUF6CGT1 is one of only four type II plant CGTs summarized. 2. It directly C-glycosylates flavones at C-6. 3. It uses apigenin and luteolin as acceptors and UDP-Glc/UDP-Gal as donors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation and di-C-glycosylation of several phenolic substrates with donor preference UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcCGT is a type I CGT capable of di-C-glycosylation. 2. It acts on 2-hydroxyflavanones, dihydrochalcone, and benzophenones. 3. It shows donor preference UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation and di-C-glycosylation of several phenolic substrates with donor preference UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CuCGT is a type I CGT capable of di-C-glycosylation. 2. It acts on 2-hydroxyflavanones, dihydrochalcone, and benzophenones. 3. It shows donor preference UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly C-glycosylates isoflavones at C-8
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLUGT43 is one of the four type II plant CGTs summarized. 2. It directly catalyzes 8-C-glycosylation of genistein and daidzein. 3. It is relevant to biosynthesis of puerarin-type metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: E. coli; plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 36 substrates including flavones, flavonols, flavanones; apigenin; luteolin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly catalyzes 8-C-glycosylation of flavonoid backbones with broad substrate promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables efficient production of orientin and vitexin; broad direct 8-C-glycosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcCGT1 is the first reported flavone 8-C-glycosyltransferase. 2. It shows high substrate promiscuity and can catalyze 8-C-glycosylation of 36 flavonoids. 3. It provided the first crystal structure of a plant CGT. 4. It is also reported as the first glycosyltransferase with C-, O-, S-, and N-glycosylation activities. 5. Using TcCGT1 as key enzyme, E. coli biosynthesized orientin and vitexin at 2324.4 and 5524.1 mg/L, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assay; crystal structure; heterologous production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UDP-bound complex structure reported; donor preference noted as UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal > UDP-Ara.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes di-C-glycosylation of flopropione-containing compounds and also shows activity toward flavones and isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgCGT catalyzes di-C-glycosylation and has broad substrate promiscuity. 2. Its di-C-glycosylation activity is associated with a spacious substrate-binding tunnel near G389. 3. Complex structures with UDP-Glc and UDP-Gal helped explain sugar-donor preference. 4. It can also show O-glycosylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assay; crystal structure; mutational/structural analysis in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conserved His-Asp catalytic dyad discussed; donor preference UDP-Glc > UDP-Xyl > UDP-Gal > UDP-Ara.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: First step of two-step di-C-glycosylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glucosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanones as first step in two-step di-C-glycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor to schaftoside/isoschaftoside-like di-C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports first step of di-C-glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnCGT1 has catalytic function similar to CGTa enzymes. 2. It glucosylates 2-hydroxyflavanones. 3. It participates in a pathway leading to schaftoside/isoschaftoside-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Second step of two-step di-C-glycosylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Arabinosylated di-C-glycosides leading to schaftoside/isoschaftoside after dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential arabinosylation of mono-C-glucosylated 2-hydroxyflavanone intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of schaftoside/isoschaftoside-like products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports second step of di-C-glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NnCGT2 has catalytic function similar to CGTb enzymes. 2. It arabinosylates mono-C-glucosylated 2-hydroxyflavanone intermediates. 3. It helps generate di-C-glycosides after dehydration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT708 family, type I CGT; membrane-bound
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regio-specific 6-C-glycosylation of coumarins and also efficiently catalyzes C-glycosylation of phloretin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCGT is a novel CGT with high regio-specificity toward coumarins. 2. It is the first characterized membrane-bound plant CGT. 3. Although functionally similar to type II for coumarins, it is classified as type I because it contains the DPF motif.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes MaCGT could efficiently catalyze C-glycosylation of phloretin as well.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly catalyzes 4-C-glycosylation of xanthones with UDP-Glc or UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N4CGT1 directly C-glycosylates xanthone. 2. It differs from MiCGT in catalytic characteristics. 3. It is classified as type I based on the DPF motif despite unusual substrate scope.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly catalyzes 4-C-glycosylation of xanthones with UDP-Glc or UDP-Gal
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N4CGT2 directly C-glycosylates xanthone. 2. It is an atypical substrate-specific member of the type I CGT group. 3. It is classified as type I because of the DPF motif.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of dihydrochalcones and uniquely prefers UDP-Xyl over UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dihydrochalcone C-glycoside production with unusual sugar-donor preference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaCGT1 catalyzes C-glycosylation of dihydrochalcones. 2. It uniquely prefers UDP-Xyl over UDP-Glc among reported plant CGTs. 3. It expands sugar-donor diversity in CGT biocatalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; diabetic mice; plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding C-glycosides including SGLT2 inhibitor candidates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of phloroglucinol-containing compounds using multiple sugar donors including UDP-Glc, TDP-Glc, UDP-Gal, UDP-Xyl, and UDP-GlcA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled synthesis of C-glycosides with hypoglycemic activity and high whole-cell production titer
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbCGT is a type I CGT from Aloe barbadensis with broad donor scope. 2. It was used to generate a series of C-glycosides as SGLT2 inhibitors. 3. Chemo-enzymatic synthesis yielded compound 46a with hypoglycemic activity in diabetic mice. 4. Whole-cell E. coli transformation achieved cumulative production of 3.95 g/L.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assay; chemo-enzymatic synthesis; whole-cell biotransformation; mouse bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for microbial production of medicinal C-glycosides and SGLT2 inhibitor analogs.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes di-C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxynaringenin and phloretin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DcaCGT is a type I CGT from Dendrobium catenatum. 2. It catalyzes di-C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxynaringenin and phloretin. 3. It expands the plant CGT catalog beyond model species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of flavone C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-glycoside biosynthesis in jujube-related pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZjCGT1 is a functional type I CGT from Ziziphus jujuba. 2. It uses 2-hydroxyflavanone as substrate. 3. It contributes to the expanding catalog of plant flavonoid CGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Plant-derived C-glycoside biotechnological production / heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of 2-hydroxyflavanone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of flavone C-glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-glycoside biosynthesis in jujube-related pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZjCGT2 is a functional type I CGT from Ziziphus jujuba. 2. It uses 2-hydroxyflavanone as substrate. 3. It contributes to the expanding catalog of plant flavonoid CGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional enzyme characterization in cited literature
